description:
A developer wants to keep meta-information about a few project files inside a
    lightweight SQLite database and to produce a very small summary report.

    Your goals:

    1. Database creation  
       • Create an SQLite database file located at  
         /home/user/project/meta.db

    2. Schema definition  
       • Inside that database create **exactly** the two tables shown below.  
         Table 1 ‑ categories  
           • name TEXT PRIMARY KEY  
         Table 2 ‑ files  
           • filename TEXT PRIMARY KEY  
           • category TEXT NOT NULL  
           • A FOREIGN-KEY constraint must reference categories(name)

    3. Data insertion  
       Insert the following rows *verbatim* (case sensitive).  
       Categories table:  
         • code  
         • docs  
       Files table:  
         • main.py            → category: code  
         • requirements.txt   → category: docs  
         • CONTRIBUTING.md    → category: docs

    4. Summary report  
       • Run the SQL query

           SELECT category, COUNT(*) AS total
           FROM   files
           GROUP  BY category
           ORDER  BY category ASC;

       • Capture **only** the returned rows (no header, no extra spacing) in a
         plain-text file located at
         /home/user/project/category_counts.log

         Exact wanted content (each line terminated by a single Unix newline):
           code|1
           docs|2

         The delimiter between the two columns must be a single vertical bar
         character “|” with **no** surrounding spaces.

    5. Verification hint  
       Automated tests will check:  
       • That /home/user/project/meta.db exists and is a valid SQLite database.  
       • That both tables exist with the correct schema.  
       • That the rows match the values listed above.  
       • That /home/user/project/category_counts.log exists with precisely the
         two lines shown, in the correct order, and **nothing else**.

    You may use any combination of shell commands and the interactive
    `sqlite3` prompt, but all SQL must end up committed into meta.db and the
    log file must follow the exact format described above.
truth:
{ "name": "code" }{ "name": "docs" }
{ "filename": "main.py", "category": "code" }{ "filename": "requirements.txt", "category": "docs" }{ "filename": "CONTRIBUTING.md", "category": "docs" }
 code|1 docs|2
